pdg_node.statement: (p: float, x: int | array<int>) -> float | array<float>
pdg_node.informal_desc: Defines and evaluates a Bernoulli distribution as a pure probabilistic oracle with no persistent mutable state.

declaration.name: bernoulli_probabilistic_oracle
declaration.type_signature: (p: float, x: int | array<int>) -> float | array<float>
declaration.docstring: Defines and evaluates a Bernoulli distribution as a pure probabilistic oracle with no persistent mutable state.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Binary_State_LogMass_Oracle. Maps a binary observation and a unit-interval parameter to the log of a two-outcome discrete mass function. The computation is closed-form and elementwise: log_mass = x * log(p) + (1 - x) * log(1 - p), with broadcasting over arrays when provided. It performs no parameter updates or internal caching, so identical inputs always produce identical outputs.. Applications: Digital communications: scoring observed bit sequences under a binary noise/channel model., Reliability engineering: evaluating pass/fail component test outcomes against hypothesized failure propensity., Computational biology: modeling binary assay readouts (activation/inactivation) under a latent success-rate parameter.
